message:
feat(ui): implement async Phase 2 analysis notification (bv-e3ub)

- Add runPhase2Analysis() method to BackgroundWorker that waits for
  Phase 2 completion and sends Phase2UpdateMsg to the UI
- Modify SnapshotBuilder.Build() to use AnalyzeAsync() for non-blocking
  initial snapshot creation
- Add Phase2Ready field to DataSnapshot to track Phase 2 status
- Add GetGraphStats() helper method to DataSnapshot
- Handle Phase2UpdateMsg in model.go Update() with DataHash validation
- Add tests for Phase 2 async behavior including race detection

This allows the UI to render immediately with Phase 1 data while
expensive Phase 2 metrics (PageRank, Betweenness, HITS, etc.) compute
in the background.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1179,6 +1179,24 @@ func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
 			m.applyFilter()
 		}
 
+	case Phase2UpdateMsg:
+		// BackgroundWorker notifies that Phase 2 analysis is complete (bv-e3ub)
+		// Verify this update matches the current snapshot using DataHash
+		if m.snapshot == nil || m.snapshot.DataHash != msg.DataHash {
+			// Stale update - ignore
+			return m, nil
+		}
+
+		// Mark snapshot as Phase 2 ready
+		m.snapshot.Phase2Ready = true
+
+		// Note: Phase2ReadyMsg handler (via WaitForPhase2Cmd) already handles
+		// all the UI updates (insights, graph view, alerts, etc.). This message
+		// is a complementary notification from the BackgroundWorker that Phase 2
+		// completed. If Phase2ReadyMsg hasn't fired yet, it will handle the full
+		// UI refresh. If it already fired (race condition), this is a no-op.
+		return m, nil
+
 	case HistoryLoadedMsg:
 		// Background history loading completed
 		m.historyLoading = false
